title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: device active, can use normal plot, grid ggplot commands. can also customize lines fills drawn, using brushes. Brushes mypaint-brushes package, can install via package manager (e.g. apt brew).  want different plot elements look different, can use set_brush() calls. set brush NULL print axis using (close ) standard R graphics:
code: set_brush("tanda/marker-01") barplot(VADeaths, beside = TRUE, col = NA, cex.names = 0.8) set_brush("experimental/bubble") barplot(VADeaths, axes = FALSE,          beside = TRUE, col = palette.colors(5), border = NA,         cex.names = 0.8) set_brush(NULL) axis(side = 2, at = seq(0, 60, 20))

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: Mypaint brushes work well mypaintr (yet). particular, smudging isn’t fully implemented yet. brushes ’ve found good use, .e. neither crazy similar standard R: classic/charcoal classic/coarse_bulk_1 (_2 _3) classic/dry_brush classic/ink_blot classic/ink_eraser classic/kabura classic/pen classic/pencil classic/slow_ink classic/textured_ink deevad/2B_pencil deevad/4H_pencil deevad/chalk deevad/spray2 Dieterle/HalfToneCMY#1 Dieterle/Pencil-_Left_Handed Dieterle/Posterizer experimental/bubble experimental/track experimental/pixelblocking experimental/sewing experimental/small_blot experimental/spaced-blot experimental/speed_blot experimental/subtle_pencil experimental/track kaerhon_v1/inkster_l ramon/2B_pencil ramon/B-pencil ramon/P._Shade ramon/Pastel_1 ramon/Pen ramon/Sketch_1 ramon/Thin_Pen tanda/acrylic-05-paint tanda/charcoal-01 tanda/charcoal-03 tanda/charcoal-04 tanda/marker-01 tanda/marker-05 tanda/oil-06-paint tanda/pencil-2b tanda/pencil-8b

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: way customize plotting set “hand”. brushes change plotted along given path, hands change path , adding jitter, multiple lines human-like quirks:   Combining brushes hands, can turn R graphics sketch.
code: set_hand(hand(bow = 0.015, wobble = 0)) barplot(VADeaths, beside = TRUE, col = NA, cex.names = 0.8) set_hand(hand(bow = 0, wobble = 0.01, multi_stroke = 2)) barplot(VADeaths, beside = TRUE, col = NA, cex.names = 0.8) set_brush("experimental/bubble") set_hand(hand(seed = 1)) filled.contour(volcano, asp = 1, plot.title = "Maunga Whau",                xlab = "Metres North", ylab = "Metres West")

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: one glitch mypaint_device: may spotted, borders fills don’t always match . , rectangle border fills plotted roughly, random roughness computed separately .  draw_rough_* functions two useful things: always fill roughly drawn shapes correctly. can used graphics device, However, note hands can work graphics device, mypaint brushes can used mypaint_device() graphics device. next chunks use knitr’s standard "png" device.  Control lines fills hand argument:
code: set_hand(hand(seed = 1)) plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n") rect(2, 2, 8, 8, col = "green4", border = "black") knitr::opts_chunk$get("dev") #> [1] "ragg_png"  plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n") draw_rough_polygons(c(2, 4, 6), c(4, 2, 6), col = "red") draw_rough_rect(8, 4, 5, 8, col = "blue3", fill_pattern = hatch())  draw_rough_arrows(1, 9, 8, 9, col = "grey40") plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n")  my_hand <- hand(wobble = 0.01, multi_stroke = 2) draw_rough_polygons(c(2, 4, 6), c(4, 2, 6), col = "red", hand = my_hand) draw_rough_rect(8, 4, 5, 8, col = "blue3", hand = my_hand, fill_pattern = hatch())  draw_rough_arrows(1, 9, 8, 9, col = "grey40", hand = my_hand) plot(c(0.01, 0.11), c(0.01, 0.11), type = "n",       xlab = "bow", ylab = "wobble",       mar = rep(0.1, 4))  for (wobble in 1:5 * 0.02) for (bow in 1:5 * 0.02) {   my_hand <- hand(wobble = wobble, bow = bow)   draw_rough_rect(     bow - 0.008, wobble - 0.008,     bow + 0.008, wobble + 0.008,     hand = my_hand,     col = "red"   ) }

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: Use fill_pattern argument fill polygon using hand-sketched lines. mypaintr knows four ways . , work base graphics devices via draw_rough_* functions:
code: plot(0:10, 0:10, type = "n")  draw_rough_rect(0, 1, 4, 5, col = "blue", fill_pattern = hatch()) draw_rough_rect(0, 6, 4, 10, col = "green4", fill_pattern = crosshatch()) draw_rough_rect(6, 1, 10, 5, col = "red3", fill_pattern = zigzag()) draw_rough_rect(6, 6, 10, 10, col = "grey30", fill_pattern = jumble()) text(c(2, 2, 8, 8), c(0.5, 5.5, 0.5, 5.5),       labels = c("hatch", "crosshatch", "zigzag", "jumble"))

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: can still use draw_rough_* functions mypaint_device active. lets use hands brushes. next chunk also shows use different brushes stroke fill:
code: plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n")  set_brush("experimental/bubble", type = "fill") set_brush(NULL, type = "stroke") my_hand <- hand(wobble = 0.01, multi_stroke = 2)  draw_rough_polygons(c(2, 4, 6), c(4, 2, 6), col = "red", hand = my_hand)  set_brush("deevad/ballpen") draw_rough_rect(8, 4, 5, 8, col = "blue3", hand = my_hand, fill_pattern = hatch())  draw_rough_arrows(1, 9, 8, 9, col = "grey40", hand = my_hand)

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: can use ggplot2 mypaint output device:  fine, can better: probably don’t want special brush render white plot background rectangle. might want “normal” elements mixed sketch elements. use brush elements part ggplot, use mypaint_wrap(). ’s picture clean background straight grid lines:  can also use special geoms geom_mypaint_bar() geom_mypaint_col(). allow use special fill patterns, like zigzag() jumble().  save output, can either use mypaint_device() dev.() usual, run ggsave("output.png", dev = mypaint_device). latter advantage can preview plot live, though won’t mypaintr customizations save .
code: library(ggplot2)  # At the console, do this: # mypaint_device("output.png")  set_hand(hand()) set_brush("experimental/bubble")  ggplot(diamonds) +    geom_bar(aes(cut, fill = cut)) +     theme_minimal() ggplot(diamonds) +    mypaint_wrap(     geom_bar(aes(cut, fill = cut)),     brush = "experimental/bubble",     hand = hand()   ) +    theme_minimal() ggplot(diamonds) +    geom_mypaint_bar(aes(cut, fill = cut, colour = cut),                     brush = "deevad/ballpen",                    fill_pattern = zigzag(density = 12),                    hand = hand(multi_stroke = 2)) +     theme_minimal()

title: Introduction to mypaintr
text: Knitr replays graphics device. make work dynamically updating device within chunks, must install special hook: chunks use mypaint_device, need set chunk options mypaint=TRUE, fig.keep="none". can set chunks like : Don’t set dev explicitly: hook . One limitation produce one plot per chunk. need , try setting following chunk options: may work, long don’t edit device options within single plot.
code: knitr::knit_hooks$set(mypaint = knitr_mypaint_hook()) knitr::opts_chunk$set(   mypaint = TRUE,   fig.keep = "none" ) dev='mypaint_device', fig.ext='png', fig.keep='high', mypaint=FALSE}

title: Authors and Citation
text: David Hugh-Jones. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hugh-Jones D (2026). mypaintr: Plot R Graphics Like Human. R package version 0.0.1, https://github.com/hughjonesd/mypaintr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mypaintr: Plot R Graphics Like a Human},   author = {David Hugh-Jones},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://github.com/hughjonesd/mypaintr}, }

title: mypaintr
text: mypaintr R package lets plot graphics human-like, sketched way, using brushes libmypaint library algorithms “rough” lines polygons. Installation: base R barplot using custom brush, plus hand-drawn axis:  ggplot using custom element:  Sketchy lines using ordinary base R device:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("hughjonesd/mypaintr") library(mypaintr)  # on the command line, do:  # mypaint_device("output.png")  set_brush("tanda/acrylic-05-paint") barplot(VADeaths, axes = FALSE,          beside = TRUE, col = palette.colors(5), border = NA,         cex.names = 0.8)  set_brush(NULL) set_hand(hand(wobble = 0)) axis(side = 2, at = seq(0, 60, 20))  # dev.off() library(ggplot2)  set_brush("experimental/bubble") ggplot(diamonds) +   geom_bar(aes(cut, fill = cut)) +   theme_minimal() +    theme(     plot.background = element_mypaint_rect(fill = "white"),     panel.grid = element_mypaint_line(colour = "grey")   )  # ggsave("output.png", device = mypaint_device) plot(1:10, 1:10, type = "n", xlab = "", ylab = "", axes = FALSE)  draw_rough_polygons(5 + 3 * sin(2*pi * 1:5/5), 5 + 3 * cos(2*pi * 1:5/5),                     border = "darkred", col = "red3", lwd = 2,                     hand = hand(multi_stroke = 3),                     fill_pattern = crosshatch())  draw_rough_arrows(8, 8.5, 5.5, 5.5, lwd = 2, hand = hand(bow = 0.05)) text(8, 9, "A pentagon", family = "Chalkboard")

title: libmypaint brush input metadata — brush_inputs
text: libmypaint brush input metadata

title: libmypaint brush input metadata — brush_inputs
code: brush_inputs()

title: libmypaint brush input metadata — brush_inputs
code: head(brush_inputs()) #>       cname      hard_min soft_min normal soft_max     hard_max #> 1  pressure  0.000000e+00        0    0.4        1 3.402823e+38 #> 2    speed1 -3.402823e+38        0    0.5        4 3.402823e+38 #> 3    speed2 -3.402823e+38        0    0.5        4 3.402823e+38 #> 4    random  0.000000e+00        0    0.5        1 1.000000e+00 #> 5    stroke  0.000000e+00        0    0.5        1 1.000000e+00 #> 6 direction  0.000000e+00        0    0.0      180 1.800000e+02 #>                                                                                                                                                                                                       tooltip #> 1      The pressure reported by the tablet. Usually between 0.0 and 1.0, but it may get larger when a pressure gain is used. If you use the mouse, it will be 0.5 when a button is pressed and 0.0 otherwise. #> 2          How fast you currently move. This can change very quickly. Try 'print input values' from the 'help' menu to get a feeling for the range; negative values are rare but possible for very low speed. #> 3                                                                                                                      Same as fine speed, but changes slower. Also look at the 'gross speed filter' setting. #> 4                                                                                                                         Fast random noise, changing at each evaluation. Evenly distributed between 0 and 1. #> 5 This input slowly goes from zero to one while you draw a stroke. It can also be configured to jump back to zero periodically while you move. Look at the 'stroke duration' and 'stroke hold time' settings. #> 6                                                                                  The angle of the stroke, in degrees. The value will stay between 0.0 and 180.0, effectively ignoring turns of 180 degrees.

title: libmypaint brush setting metadata — brush_settings
text: libmypaint brush setting metadata

title: libmypaint brush setting metadata — brush_settings
code: brush_settings()

title: libmypaint brush setting metadata — brush_settings
code:
head(brush_settings()) #>                cname              name min default max constant #> 1             opaque           Opacity   0     1.0   2    FALSE #> 2    opaque_multiply  Opacity multiply   0     0.0   2    FALSE #> 3   opaque_linearize Opacity linearize   0     0.9   2     TRUE #> 4 radius_logarithmic            Radius  -2     2.0   6    FALSE #> 5           hardness          Hardness   0     0.8   1    FALSE #> 6      anti_aliasing     Pixel feather   0     1.0   5    FALSE #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 tooltip #> 1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       0 means brush is transparent, 1 fully visible
(also known as alpha or opacity) #> 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      This gets multiplied with opaque. You should only change the pressure input of this setting. Use 'opaque' instead to make opacity depend on speed.
This setting is responsible to stop painting when there is zero pressure. This is just a convention, the behaviour is identical to 'opaque'. #> 3 Correct the nonlinearity introduced by blending multiple dabs on top of each other. This correction should get you a linear ("natural") pressure response when pressure is mapped to opaque_multiply, as it is usually done. 0.9 is good for standard strokes, set it smaller if your brush scatters a lot, or higher if you use dabs_per_second.
0.0 the opaque value above is for the individual dabs
1.0 the opaque value above is for the final brush stroke, assuming each pixel gets (dabs_per_radius*2) brushdabs on average during a stroke #> 4                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Basic brush radius (logarithmic)
 0.7 means 2 pixels
 3.0 means 20 pixels #> 5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Hard brush-circle borders (setting to zero will draw nothing). To reach the maximum hardness, you need to disable Pixel feather. #> 6                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         This setting decreases the hardness when necessary to prevent a pixel staircase effect (aliasing) by making the dab more blurred.
 0.0 disable (for very strong erasers and pixel brushes)
 1.0 blur one pixel (good value)
 5.0 notable blur, thin strokes will disappear

title: Cross-hatch fill pattern — crosshatch
text: Cross-hatch fill pattern

title: Cross-hatch fill pattern — crosshatch
code: crosshatch(angle = 45, density = 7, clip = TRUE, padding = 0)

title: Cross-hatch fill pattern — crosshatch
text: angle One two hatch angles degrees. single angle supplied, second pass defaults angle + 90. density Approximate line density lines per inch. Larger values give denser fills. clip TRUE, hatch endpoints stay shape boundary reduce overshoot. padding Inset polygon edge inches. Positive values leave small gap fill pattern boundary.

title: Cross-hatch fill pattern — crosshatch
text: fill-pattern object draw_rough_*() helpers mypaint geoms.

title: Cross-hatch fill pattern — crosshatch
code: plot.new() plot.window(xlim = c(0, 10), ylim = c(0, 10)) draw_rough_rect(   2, 2, 8, 8,   col = "grey90",   fill_pattern = crosshatch(angle = c(30, 120), density = 9) )

title: Draw rough, brush-rendered bars in ggplot2 — geom_mypaint_bar
code: geom_mypaint_bar(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   stat = "count",   position = "stack",   ...,   just = 0.5,   lineend = "butt",   linejoin = "mitre",   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE,   fill_pattern = NULL,   brush = NULL,   fill_brush = NULL,   hand = NULL,   stroke_hand = hand,   fill_hand = hand,   auto_solid_bg = NULL )

title: Draw rough, brush-rendered bars in ggplot2 — geom_mypaint_bar
text: mapping, data, position, just, lineend, linejoin, na.rm, show.legend, inherit.aes ggplot2::geom_col(). stat statistical transformation use. Defaults "count". ... arguments passed ggplot2::layer(). fill_pattern Optional fill pattern created hatch(), crosshatch(), zigzag(), jumble(). brush Stroke brush specification created tweak_brush(), installed mypaint brush name, .myb file path, JSON brush string, NULL solid borders. fill_brush Fill brush specification created tweak_brush(), installed mypaint brush name, .myb file path, JSON brush string, NULL solid fills. hand Optional hand-drawn geometry applied outline hatch default. stroke_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry outline. fill_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry hatch strokes. auto_solid_bg Reserved future parity device-level style controls.

title: Hand-drawn geometry settings — hand

title: Hand-drawn geometry settings — hand
text: seed Optional random seed used repeatable geometry. bow Typical bowing long strokes proportion segment length. wobble Low-frequency path wobble proportion segment length. multi_stroke Number overdrawn strokes use. width_jitter Relative variation line width overdrawn strokes. endpoint_jitter Relative endpoint jitter proportion segment length.

title: Hand-drawn geometry settings — hand
text: object describing rough geometry generated.

title: Hand-drawn geometry settings — hand
code: hand() #> $seed #> NULL #>  #> $bow #> [1] 0.015 #>  #> $wobble #> [1] 0.006 #>  #> $multi_stroke #> [1] 1 #>  #> $width_jitter #> [1] 0.08 #>  #> $endpoint_jitter #> [1] 0 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mypaintr_hand" hand(seed = 1, bow = 0.02, wobble = 0.01) #> $seed #> [1] 1 #>  #> $bow #> [1] 0.02 #>  #> $wobble #> [1] 0.01 #>  #> $multi_stroke #> [1] 1 #>  #> $width_jitter #> [1] 0.08 #>  #> $endpoint_jitter #> [1] 0 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "mypaintr_hand"

title: Hatch fill pattern — hatch
text: angle Base hatch angle degrees. density Approximate line density lines per inch. Larger values give denser fills. clip TRUE, hatch endpoints stay shape boundary reduce overshoot. padding Inset polygon edge inches. Positive values leave small gap fill pattern boundary.

title: Jumble fill pattern — jumble
text: Jumble fill pattern

title: Jumble fill pattern — jumble
code: jumble(   angle = 0,   density = 5,   radius = 0.76/density,   wobble = 0.2,   clip = TRUE,   padding = 0 )

title: Jumble fill pattern — jumble
text: angle Base angle degrees underlying guide lines. density Approximate line density lines per inch. Larger values give denser fills. radius Loop radius inches. Defaults 0.76 / density, loops sized fraction line spacing. wobble Amount irregularity loop shapes, spacing, size. Larger values give less even, varied loops. clip TRUE, split loop paths shape boundary. padding Inset polygon edge inches. Positive values leave small gap fill pattern boundary.

title: Jumble fill pattern — jumble
text: fill-pattern object draw_rough_*() helpers mypaint geoms.

title: Jumble fill pattern — jumble
code: plot.new() plot.window(xlim = c(0, 10), ylim = c(0, 10)) draw_rough_rect(2, 2, 8, 8, col = "grey90", fill_pattern = jumble())

what: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
text: returned hook opens mypaint_device() chunk evaluation injects generated PNG files afterward. avoids knitr's normal plot replay path, preserve device-local style changes set_hand() set_brush().

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
code: knitr_mypaint_hook(...)

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
text: ... Default arguments passed mypaint_device() hook opens device. Chunk-specific overrides can supplied chunk option mypaint.args named list. arguments applied device opens, use hook mypaint.args set chunk defaults; set_brush() set_hand() still work within chunk device open.

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
text: function suitable knitr::knit_hooks$set().

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
text: Register knitr::knit_hooks$set(mypaint = knitr_mypaint_hook(...)) enable chunks mypaint = TRUE. Chunks also set fig.keep = "none" fig.ext = "png". chunk explicitly sets dev=, hook skipped knitr's normal device handling used.

title: Create a knitr chunk hook for live mypaint rendering — knitr_mypaint_hook
code: if (requireNamespace("knitr", quietly = TRUE)) {   hook <- knitr_mypaint_hook(brush = "deevad/2B_pencil")   print(is.function(hook)) } #> [1] TRUE

what: Load an installed mypaint brush — load_brush
title: Load an installed mypaint brush — load_brush
text: Load installed mypaint brush

title: Load an installed mypaint brush — load_brush
code: load_brush(brush, paths = default_mypaint_brush_dirs(), normalize = "all")

title: Load an installed mypaint brush — load_brush
text: brush Brush name like "classic/pencil" path .myb file. paths Optional brush directories. Defaults locally discovered mypaint-brushes locations. normalize One "", "size", "tracking", "none". Defaults ""; use "none" explicitly bypass normalization.

title: Load an installed mypaint brush — load_brush
text: reusable brush specification object.

title: Load an installed mypaint brush — load_brush
code: if (length(brushes())) {   x <- load_brush(brushes()[[1]])   stopifnot(inherits(x, "mypaintr_brush")) }

what: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
text: Open libmypaint-backed graphics device

title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
code: mypaint_device(   filename = NULL,   file = NULL,   width = 7,   height = 7,   res = 144,   pointsize = 12,   bg = "white",   brush = NULL,   stroke_style = NULL,   fill_style = NULL,   fill_brush = NULL,   hand = NULL,   stroke_hand = NULL,   fill_hand = NULL,   auto_solid_bg = TRUE )

title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
text: filename Output PNG filename. contains \%d, pages numbered. file Deprecated compatibility alias filename. width, height Device size inches. res Resolution pixels per inch. pointsize Base pointsize. bg Background colour. brush Stroke brush specification created tweak_brush(), installed mypaint brush name, .myb file path, JSON brush string, NULL solid strokes. omitted, mypaint_device() uses internal default plotting brush. stroke_style Legacy override whether stroke drawing uses brush backend solid Cairo rendering. NULL, inferred whether brush NULL. fill_style Legacy override whether fill drawing uses brush backend solid Cairo rendering. NULL, inferred whether fill_brush NULL. fill_brush Optional fill brush spec. Defaults brush supplied. Use explicit NULL solid fills. hand Optional hand-drawn geometry spec applied stroke fill primitives default. stroke_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry spec strokes. fill_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry spec fills. auto_solid_bg Draw large fills match device background using normal Cairo rendering even fill_style = "brush".

title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
text: Opens graphics device returns NULL invisibly.

title: Open a libmypaint-backed graphics device — mypaint_device
code: out <- tempfile("mypaint-basic-", fileext = "-%d.png") mypaint_device(out, width = 4, height = 3, bg = "ivory") plot(   1:10,   col = "steelblue",   pch = 16,   cex = 1.4,   main = "Ink Lines" ) lines(1:10, col = "firebrick", lwd = 3) rect(2, 3, 5, 7, border = "black", col = rgb(0, 0.6, 0.3, 0.25)) text(6, 8, "hello", col = "black") dev.off() #> agg_record_35242802b32  #>                      2  unlink(Sys.glob(sub("%d", "*", out, fixed = TRUE)))  if ("classic/pen" %in% brushes()) {   out <- tempfile("mypaint-brush-", fileext = "-%d.png")   mypaint_device(     out,     width = 4,     height = 3,     brush = tweak_brush("classic/pen", tracking_noise = 0.12),     fill_style = "brush",     fill_brush = tweak_brush("classic/pen", normalize = "size", radius_by_random = 0.08)   )   plot.new()   plot.window(xlim = c(0, 10), ylim = c(0, 10))   polygon(     c(2, 5, 8, 6, 3),     c(2, 7, 6, 3, 1.5),     border = "black",     col = rgb(0.2, 0.7, 0.5, 0.6)   )   lines(c(1, 9), c(1, 9), col = "firebrick", lwd = 4)   title("Brush Fill")   box()   dev.off()   unlink(Sys.glob(sub("%d", "*", out, fixed = TRUE))) }  out <- tempfile("mypaint-mixed-", fileext = "-%d.png") mypaint_device(out, width = 4, height = 3, brush = NULL) plot(1:5, 1:5, type = "n", main = "Mixed Styles") if ("classic/pencil" %in% brushes()) {   set_brush("classic/pencil", type = "stroke") } lines(1:5, c(1, 3, 2, 5, 4), lwd = 3) dev.off() #> agg_record_35242802b32  #>                      2  unlink(Sys.glob(sub("%d", "*", out, fixed = TRUE)))

what: Wrap a grid grob, ggplot layer, or ggplot theme element with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
title: Wrap a grid grob, ggplot layer, or ggplot theme element with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
text: mypaint_wrap() applies temporary mypaintr brush hand settings wrapped object drawn, restores previous device style. can wrap grid grobs, ggplot2 layers, ggplot2 theme elements. makes useful direct grid::grid.draw() workflows, ggplot calls ggplot(...) + mypaint_wrap(geom_line(...), ...), theme elements theme(panel.grid = mypaint_wrap(element_line(), ...)).

title: Wrap a grid grob, ggplot layer, or ggplot theme element with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
code: mypaint_wrap(   object,   brush = NULL,   fill_brush = NULL,   hand = NULL,   stroke_hand = hand,   fill_hand = hand,   auto_solid_bg = NULL )

title: Wrap a grid grob, ggplot layer, or ggplot theme element with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
text: object grid grob, ggplot2 layer, ggplot2 theme element. brush Stroke brush specification created tweak_brush(), installed mypaint brush name, .myb file path, JSON brush string, NULL solid strokes. fill_brush Fill brush specification created tweak_brush(), installed mypaint brush name, .myb file path, JSON brush string, NULL solid fills. hand Optional hand-drawn geometry applied stroke fill default. stroke_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry strokes. fill_hand Optional hand-drawn geometry fills. auto_solid_bg Optional override background-like fills.

title: Wrap a grid grob, ggplot layer, or ggplot theme element with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
text: object general kind object.

title: Wrap a grid grob, ggplot layer, or ggplot theme element with scoped mypaint styling — mypaint_wrap
code: line <- grid::linesGrob(c(0.1, 0.9), c(0.2, 0.8)) if ("classic/pen" %in% brushes()) {   wrapped <- mypaint_wrap(line, brush = "classic/pen", hand = hand()) }  if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE) &&     "classic/pen" %in% brushes()) {   ggplot2::ggplot(mtcars, ggplot2::aes(wt, mpg)) +     mypaint_wrap(ggplot2::geom_line(), brush = "classic/pen", hand = hand())    ggplot2::theme(     panel.grid = mypaint_wrap(ggplot2::element_line(), brush = "classic/pen")   ) } #> <theme> List of 1 #>  $ panel.grid: <mypaintr_element_line> #>   ..@ colour       : NULL #>   ..@ linewidth    : NULL #>   ..@ linetype     : NULL #>   ..@ lineend      : NULL #>   ..@ linejoin     : NULL #>   ..@ arrow        : logi FALSE #>   ..@ arrow.fill   : NULL #>   ..@ inherit.blank: logi FALSE #>  @ complete: logi FALSE #>  @ validate: logi TRUE

title: Brush paths parameter — mypaintr-brush-paths-param
text: paths Optional brush directories. Defaults locally discovered mypaint-brushes locations.

title: Fill pattern parameters — mypaintr-fill-pattern-params
text: Fill pattern parameters

title: Fill pattern parameters — mypaintr-fill-pattern-params
text: angle Base hatch angle degrees. density Approximate line density lines per inch. Larger values give denser fills. padding Inset polygon edge inches. Positive values leave small gap fill pattern boundary. clip TRUE, hatch endpoints stay shape boundary reduce overshoot.

what: mypaintr: Plot R Graphics Like a Human — mypaintr-package
title: mypaintr: Plot R Graphics Like a Human — mypaintr-package
text: R graphics device human-like, sketched plotting. Uses libmypaint brushes draw lines polygons. Includes functions plotting "rough" lines polygons. Integrates base "ggplot" graphics.

title: Set the active mypaintr hand-drawn geometry — set_hand
text: hand Hand-drawn geometry created hand(), NULL disable selected type. disables rough path perturbation ; disable active brush, note brushes internal wobbly pathing! Use set_brush() well want fully plain, solid rendering. type rendering channel update: "", "stroke", "fill".
